slots.words: Most people stop reading Psalm twenty-two at the cross.
slots.note: Empty rock-hewn tomb with burial ledge and dawn light, no figure — matches the empty-tomb hook.
Open Hebrew Psalm scroll, lower lines catching light, faint background vignettes — matches 'the psalm turns'.

slots.words: the same voice moves from anguish to praise:
slots.note: Aged David with scroll lifting his face in hope, risen Christ in white at the divide — matches the anguish-to-praise turn.

slots.words: "I will declare thy name unto my brethren:
slots.note: Weathered fingertip beneath a line of ancient Hebrew on cracked parchment — matches 'I will declare thy name'.

slots.words: in the midst of the congregation will I praise thee."
slots.note: Risen Christ at the centre of a gathered group of men and women, hand lifted — matches 'in the midst of the congregation'.
Close view of the risen Christ's living eyes and the faint thorn-scar at the brow — matches 'who is that praising voice?'.
Macro of an ancient codex / manuscript edge — the book under 'Hebrews takes that opening line'; relevant, no foreign story.

slots.words: and puts it in the mouth of the risen Christ.
slots.note: The risen Christ, living bearded face, white robe — matches 'in the mouth of the risen Christ'.

slots.words: alive on the far side of the cross.
slots.note: Two open palms with healed nail-marks raised, the living face above — matches 'alive on the far side of the cross'.
Risen Christ drawing a kneeling man into an embrace, hand on his shoulder — matches 'not ashamed to call them brethren'.
The risen Christ's scarred open hand, healed nail-mark visible, resting on a weathered man's shoulder — matches 'brothers'.
Hands reaching to welcome, meeting an open hand among the gathered — matches 'Family'.

slots.words: and the risen Christ is not ashamed to call His own brethren.
slots.note: Risen Christ's living face turned warmly to the viewer — matches 'the risen Christ is not ashamed to call His own brethren'.
